data.title: High-Yield REITs For Volatile Markets: Yielding 4.7%
data.description:
REITs: A Shield in Stormy Markets

Keep calm and carry on: advice that’s easier said than done as back-and-forth tariff policies, escalating trade wars, and stubborn inflation continue to hammer markets. Historically, it’s no surprise that REITs can be a defensive safe haven during periods of market uncertainty and volatility. REITs serve as a defensive safe haven during market turbulence due to their stable dividend income, tangible asset backing, and inflation-hedging properties. Their required high dividend payouts provide consistent cash flow, while real estate holdings help preserve value. Certain sectors, like healthcare and residential REITs, offer steady demand, making them less vulnerable to economic downturns. Additionally, REITs often have a lower correlation to equities, enhancing portfolio diversification. Though sensitive to interest rates, their ability to adjust rents with inflation and generate passive income makes them a resilient option during market volatility.

This time around is no exception. Several weeks ago, I wrote about the importance of maintaining a barbell approach in turbulent times: combining fundamentally strong stocks with income-generating dividend stocks can provide a steady income stream and mitigate risk. When it comes to the first part of that strategy, Seeking Alpha’s Quant System can help identify Strong Buy-rated stocks that are well-positioned for strong returns once conditions stabilize (watch our latest video on top stocks to take on Trump tariffs here.) As for the second part, investors should prioritize capital preservation by focusing on dividend-paying stocks with strong fundamentals.

REITs for Downside Protection with Upside Potential

Real estate investment trusts ((REITs)) can be a great way to do just that. Targeting REITs with solid yields and strong dividend safety—two critical investment characteristics in a market sell-off—is an easy way to hedge against volatility. There are also strong tailwinds blowing toward the sector. REITs could get a boost if the Federal Reserve becomes more supportive of lower interest rates this year, a scenario looking increasingly more likely as recession fears mount and market turmoil chips away at consumer confidence. Since June 2007, the iShares Core US REIT ETF (USRT) has generally moved in the opposite direction of the 10-year Treasury yield—meaning REITs tend to perform better when interest rates fall. The strongest gains for USRT occurred when the 10-year yield dropped between 0.10 and 0.25 percentage points in a month. However, when yields fell sharply, REIT performance weakened, likely due to increased economic concerns.

Future traders are widely betting that the next interest rate will occur in June, with 30-Day Fed Funds tracking at an 89.1% probability that the central bank will ease rates, according to the CME FedWatch tool. Policymakers indicated in December that it would cut rates only twice this year, but traders are betting on even more.

Future Traders Bet on an 89.1% probability that the Fed will lower rates in June

That could be a positive development for REITs. A new era of cheap money means real estate developers can more easily refinance debt, free up cash flow, and embark on new projects. Whether you’re bullish on more future rate cuts or not, REITs offer solid downside protection right now in addition to upside potential.

Using SA’s Quant System, I’ve selected five high-quality, diversified REITs offering above-average yields, solid safety grades, and solid AFFO and FFO growth. With an average dividend yield of 4.7%, these REITs make great options for income-oriented investors and growth investors seeking more stability in the current environment.

Income-Generating REITs: Yielding 4.7% on Average (as of 3/12/25)

They include Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. (BNL), Cousins Properties Incorporated (NYSE: CUZ), Alexander & Baldwin, Inc. (ALEX), Regency Centers Corporation (REG), and VICI Properties Inc. (VICI).

How We Scan for Top REITs

To screen for the best REITs on the market, SA Quant analyzes the full real estate investment universe using powerful computer processing and our special REIT ‘Quantamental’ analysis. It then ranks the REITs with the strongest collective value, growth, profitability, risk, and price momentum metrics vs. the broader REIT sector as well as multiples and payout ratios specific to REITs, including FFO, AFFO, FAD, Gross Properties, and more. Over the past 6 years, the backtested strategy has delivered impressive returns, more than doubling Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLRE) as of March 10, 2025.

Seeking Alpha’s REIT Recommendations Eclipse the Real Estate Sector

From these results, I’ve identified five stocks that exhibit excellent income-generating characteristics in addition to the following criteria:

  1. Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  2. Market capitalization > 500M

  3. Stock price >$2.00

  4. Solid dividend yield

  5. Positive factor grades across the board

The stocks below represent an attractive blend of income-yielding characteristics, as well as other positive SA Quant Factor Grades and Quant Dividend Grades. To see all of the ‘Strong Buy’ recommendations, go to the Top REITs Quant Screener.

1. Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. (NYSE:BNL)

  • Market Capitalization: $3.35B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 2 out of 173

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 1 out of 14

  • Dividend Yield (FWD): 6.86%

  • Dividend Safety Grade: C+

The top Quant-rated Diversified REIT Stock and ranked second across all Real Estate Stocks, Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. is a REIT offering commercial net lease properties across the industrial sector, restaurants, retail, healthcare, and office properties. With a longstanding track record of delivering attractive risk-adjusted returns, Broadstone trades at an attractive valuation to its peers while boasting an industry-leading 6.84% (FWD) dividend yield and strong fundamentals across the board.

While the group’s lukewarm Q4 2024 results failed to wow investors, BNL achieved $1.43 AFFO per share for 2024 while securing $400M in total investments last year, indicating that it’s rearing up for more growth in 2025. The company anticipates to report AFFO of between $1.45 and $1.49 per diluted share this year.

BNL Stock Dividend Breakdown

Up around 14% over the past year and 9% over the past month, BNL continues to climb even as the broader stock market is getting crushed, riding high on its attractive dividend yield and forward price-to-FFO ratio of 10.55x, among other income-generating characteristics.

BNL Dividend Scorecard (as of 3/12/25)

With one and three-year yield-on-cost ratios of 7.8% and 5.4%, respectively, BNL has demonstrated a fantastic dividend yield with an overall ‘A-’ in the category. Analysts predict it will continue to grow in the next few years, with a 7.33% consensus ratio for December 2026, making it a compelling choice for value-inclined investors.

BNL also comes at an attractive price tag. Its forward price-to-funds from operations (P/FFO) ratio of 11 represents an 18.4% discount to the sector median, while its forward price-to-adjusted funds from operations (P/AFFO) ratio of 11.5 comes at a 23.6% discount. SA analyst Pearl Gray Equity and Research notes:

“Despite a decline in FFO per share, largely due to asset sales, Broadstone's forward price-to-FFO ratio of 10.55x and 7.46% dividend yield remain attractive.”

BNL’s dividend payments have a reliable track record, with 4 years of consistent dividend growth—a 950% difference to the sector—and a three-year CAGR dividend growth rate of 4%. The REIT has also demonstrated solid profitability with an AFFO/Total Revenue (TTM) ratio of nearly 70%, a 73% difference from the sector, and a gross profit margin (TTM) of 94%.

My next selection offers a similarly attractive dividend yield with dividend safety characteristics to help counter tumultuous markets.

2. Cousins Properties Incorporated (NYSE:CUZ)

  • Market Capitalization: $4.7B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 3 out of 173

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 1 out of 20

  • Dividend Yield (FWD): 4.5%

  • Dividend Safety Grade: B+

Founded in 1958 in Atlanta, GA, Cousins Properties focuses on developing, owning, and managing office buildings in high-growth markets across the Sun Belt region of the United States. Targeting high-quality assets with luxury amenities, CUZ’s properties command higher rent than the average property manager, with on-site retail and food, outdoor spaces, and fitness centers. Currently, the top Quant-ranked Office REIT, CUZ had a successful fourth quarter, beating EPS estimates by $0.02 and raising its 2025 FFO guidance to $2.73 to $2.83 per share. The company has begun to bounce back from low office occupancies fuelled by Covid and the shift to remote work, reporting 89.2% occupancy in the fourth quarter, up from 87.6% at the end of 2023.

CUZ Stock Dividend Breakdown

CUZ maintains strong dividend safety, with a forward FFO payout ratio of 47.6%, a -28.8% difference from the sector, and an AFFO payout ratio of 73%, a -2.7% difference broadly in line with the sector. CUZ is currently trading at 10.2 forward P/FFO ratio, a -24.6% discount to the sector median, and a 24.8% net debt long-to-assets ratio, a -37.3% difference. It has also demonstrated solid momentum over the past 12 months, earning an overall ‘A-’ in the category with a one-year price performance of 19.2. Despite being down 5.6% year-to-date, analysts have expressed optimism, with three up revisions in the last 90 days and zero down revisions.

CUZ Dividend Scorecard (as of 3/12/25)

Dividend consistency could use some work, notching 0 years of consistent dividend growth despite delivering 7 consecutive years of dividend payments. CUZ’s combination of steady growth, strong momentum, dividend safety, and discounted valuation make it a solid choice for income-inclined investors.

My next pick offers a similar blend of attractive Quant Factor and Dividend grades.

3. Alexander & Baldwin, Inc. (NYSE:ALEX)

  • Market Capitalization: $1.3B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 6 out of 173

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 2 out of 14

  • Dividend Yield (FWD): 4.9%

  • Dividend Safety Grade: B-

Ranked 2 out of 14 among Diversified REITS and 5th in the Real Estate sector overall, ALEX is a diversified REIT based in Honolulu, Hawaii focused solely on its home state with properties ranging from retail, office centers, industrial assets, and 142 acres of ground leases. A combination of strong financial performance and a near 5% dividend yield has helped it gain more than 10% over the past year and stay in the green during the past month’s market tumult. The island REIT reported fourth-quarter FFO of $0.30 per share, a $0.03 year-over-year increase, and AFFO of $0.19 per share, a $0.02 increase—improvements fueled primarily by stronger performance by its commercial real estate portfolio. The 153-year-old company is in the midst of monetizing its remaining non-core assets, efforts that were reflected by an increase in Q4 operating profits to 22M and an FFO to gross margin (TTM) ratio of 91%, a 42.6% difference from the sector. As Seeking Alpha analyst Gen Alpha highlights in this analysis, ALEX demonstrates solid FFO growth, high occupancy rates, and a well-covered dividend, making it a compelling choice for income investors.

ALEX Stock Dividend Breakdown

ALEX’s 4.9% dividend yield is backed by a 77% payout ratio, a 5-year CAGR of 5.3%, and forward AFFO growth of 7.7%, a 182% difference from the 2.75% sector median. The stock appears fairly valued compared to its peers, with a P/FFO ratio of 15.9.

ALEX Dividend Scorecard (as of 3/12/25)

The Hawaiian REIT also exhibits solid dividend safety characteristics, including an FFO interest rate coverage ratio of 5.24, a near 80% difference vs. the sector’s 2.9. Analysts have expressed confidence in its continued success, with a 4.9% consensus ratio for December 2026. Dividend growth is also attractive, with forward AFFO growth of 7.7%, a 182% difference compared to the sector.

Like ALEX, my next pick combines solid financials with high dividend growth and safety grades to help protect portfolios in the current drawdown.

4. Regency Centers Corporation (NASDAQ:REG)

  • Market Capitalization: $13.5B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 7 out of 173

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 1 out of 23

  • Dividend Yield (FWD): 3.8%

  • Dividend Safety Grade: B+

The top Quant-ranked Retail REIT, Regency Centers Corporation owns over 470 retail properties across the U.S. with a particular focus on grocery stores, housing top brands including Whole Foods, Publix, Safeway, and Trader Joes. Returning nearly 20% over the past year and remaining flat YTD, REG is weathering the stock market storm with a strong balance sheet and resilient, geographically diversified business model that prioritizes high-quality supermarket chains in affluent areas. Its 3.8% dividend yield offers dependable income and modest growth, backed by strong Quant Dividend grades.

Its modest dividend yield is well-insulated by its payout ratio of 62% and a 3% five-year growth rate. Analysts estimate that number will continue to benefit from growth in the real estate sector, with a consensus yield of 4.05% for December 2027 and five upward EPS revisions over the past 90 days.

REG Dividend Scorecard (as of 3/12/25)

REG Stock Dividend Breakdown

While REG may be a little more on the expensive side with an overall ‘C-’ Valuation Grade, the REIT touts excellent momentum with one-year price performance coming in at a 522% difference to the sector median. Profitability is also solid with FFO to gross margin (TTM) of 74%, a 15.7% difference to the sector median. Perhaps its biggest selling point is its ‘A’ Dividend Growth Grade, boosted by an ‘A+’ dividend growth rate (TTM) of 30.6%--an impressive 2,044.6% to the 1.43% sector median. FFO growth of 4.4%, a 63% difference, and AFFO growth of 3.8%, a 39.3% difference, also underscore REG’s potential as a long-term investment. Strong Dividend Safety metrics also support future dividend growth estimates.

Like REG, my next selection reflects strong safety and growth characteristics that have fuelled analysts’ bullish expectations for an even higher dividend yield.

5. VICI Properties Inc. (NYSE:VICI)

  • Market Capitalization: $1.64B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 1 out of 179

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/12/25): 1 out of 25

  • Dividend Yield (FWD): 5.31%

  • Dividend Safety Grade: B+

One of my top 3 high-yielding dividend stocks for 2025, VICI Properties counts Caesars Palace and MGM Grand among its portfolio of Vegas trophy assets and other commercial property assets. Gaming sector REITs boast long lease terms and above-average rent escalators, as reflected by VICI’s 100% occupancy rate and highly profitable triple-net lease model. The company has risen over 8% over the past month and 10.7% YTD, making this an investment that could be worth rolling the dice on even when the rest of the market is down on its luck. Despite missing Wall Street expectations with its Q4 FFO estimates, VICI has demonstrated solid revenue growth and annual AFFO per share growth of 5.1% to $0.57 in Q4 2024 from $0.55 in Q4 2023.

VICI Stock Dividend Breakdown

The triple-net REIT’s 5.3% dividend yield is really what keeps investors coming back to the table, backed by a 65% payout ratio,7.7% five-year growth rate and six years of consecutive dividend growth—a 1,475% difference to the sector that underpins its ‘A-’ Dividend Consistency Grade. Analysts are betting that its dividend will continue to grow, with a consensus yield of 5.8% for December 2027.

VICI Dividend Scorecard (as of 3/12/25)

VICI is undervalued, as highlighted by its overall B+ Quant Grade, which is supported by a forward P/AFFO of 12.71 versus the sector’s 15.01, more than a 15% discount, and a forward P/FFO of 10.86, more than a 15.4% discount. It also exhibits ‘A’-rated AFFO growth of 6.29%, a 320.2% difference from the sector median, and ‘A+’-rated FFO growth of 28.1%, a 1,400.2% difference from the 1.9% sector median.

With its juicy dividend yield and a healthy mix of strong valuation and growth metrics, VICI just might be worth the gamble.

Conclusion

Sliding stocks have rung alarm bells across Wall Street, but there’s no need to panic. Utilizing my tried and true barbell approach of seeking out stocks with strong fundamentals as well as more dependable, income-generating stocks such as the five REITs in this article will help diversify your portfolio and protect your existing investments. Feel free to explore SA Quants other Top REITs.

We have many stocks with strong buy recommendations, and you can filter them using stock screens to suit your specific investment objectives. Consider using Seeking Alpha's 'Ratings Screener' tool to help find stocks that achieve diversification into desired sectors you like. Alternatively, if you're looking for a select number of Quant Strong Buy recommendations on a monthly basis, you might want to explore Alpha Picks.
